chats.bubbleTitle: Truth is discovered through lived testing
chats.excerpt: A core philosophical thread is that ideas become trustworthy through lived testing, not just abstract consistency. The user returns to this often as a criterion for truth, discernment, and moral seriousness.
One recurring open problem is whether memory continuity is enough for personal identity, or whether identity has a deeper spiritual or metaphysical center beyond remembered experience.
Reference material connecting personhood, soul, embodiment, and moral agency. Keep this as a reusable source bubble instead of a settled conclusion.
